decision: Delivery port method is deliver(content: str) -> None, consuming the rendered Markdown string from S5
reasoning: S5 render(...) -> str produces a string not a Digest; renderer-5 handoff states S6 consumes the returned string; keeps S6 decoupled from domain models

decision: File delivery is atomic: write temp file in target dir -> fsync -> os.replace
reasoning: architecture.md mandates write-temp-then-rename so a crash never leaves a partial/corrupt digest; matches state store V1

decision: All delivered output is UTF-8 encoded
reasoning: digest contains Vietnamese labels (Khac bucket) and possibly emoji/CJK titles; platform default encoding (cp1252) would corrupt it

decision: stdout mode writes the digest (+1 trailing newline) to sys.stdout only; no file written; output_path ignored
reasoning: PROJECT_SPEC §5 V1 file and/or stdout; clean-pipe Unix convention (logs to stderr)
rejected: also write a file in stdout mode

decision: Destination is config-driven via [output] section: destination (file|stdout, default file), output_path (default ./digest.md); Config gains output_destination + output_path
reasoning: watch_item requires config-driven selection without inventing keys silently; mirrors existing optional [state] section pattern

decision: Invalid [output].destination value raises ConfigError at load_config (fail fast), not at delivery time
reasoning: conventions.md fail-fast on bad config before pipeline runs

decision: Missing parent directory for output_path is an error (Error: on stderr, exit 1); Delivery does NOT auto-create it
reasoning: fail-fast convention; avoids creating surprise directory trees

decision: Unwritable file destination (permission denied / is-a-directory / disk full) surfaces Error: <message> on stderr + non-zero exit, no stacktrace
reasoning: conventions.md CLI error contract; fail loud to avoid silent data loss

decision: Delivery overwrites the target deterministically and never appends (idempotent re-run)
reasoning: PROJECT_SPEC §7 idempotency: re-running must not produce duplicate/partial output

decision: Delivery imports no upstream pipeline modules (github, summarizer, cache, render) and depends on no domain model
reasoning: architecture.md hard boundary: S6 is a decoupled sink operating only on the string

decision: Change ports.py Delivery Protocol from send(self, digest: Digest) -> None to deliver(self, content: str) -> None (MODIFIED capability)
reasoning: the old stub never matched the realized S5 string contract; architect must update the Protocol and any wiring stub

decision: Change Delivery Protocol send(self, digest: Digest) -> None to deliver(self, content: str) -> None; Digest model untouched
reasoning: S5 render(...) emits str not Digest (locked D-1); keeps S6 decoupled from domain models; push-channel-agnostic for V2
rejected: keep send(Digest) and rebuild Digest in S6

decision: Atomic file write reuses state-store-3 pattern: tempfile.mkstemp(dir=target.parent) -> os.fdopen(encoding=utf-8) write+flush+os.fsync -> os.replace -> finally unlink; NO mkdir
reasoning: RF-1 same-fs atomic rename; proven in json_store.save; diverges from state store by NOT auto-creating the parent (AC-6-014 fail-fast)

decision: BrokenPipeError handled at CLI top level (os.dup2 stdout->devnull + clean exit), NOT inside StdoutDelivery
reasoning: the broken pipe can fire on the interpreter final flush of sys.stdout after deliver returns; only a top-level handler reliably suppresses the stacktrace (AC-6-009, RF-5); adapter stays a dumb writer
rejected: catch inside StdoutDelivery.deliver only (misses late flush, fails | head case)

decision: [output] validated fail-fast in config.py:load_config mirroring the [state] step; Config gains output_destination + output_path
reasoning: invalid destination/empty output_path must fail before the pipeline runs (BR-6-008); reuses ConfigError->CLI Error: surface

decision: No openapi.yaml for this change (CLI tool, no inbound HTTP API)
reasoning: R-API-003 deviation justified: no HTTP surface; the contract is a Python Protocol + [output] config; cites collector ADR-007, state ADR-004, renderer ADR-005
rejected: author a stub openapi.yaml fabricating a non-existent HTTP API

decision: DeliveryError in src/osspulse/delivery/errors.py mirrors StateError; file-write OSError re-raised as DeliveryError naming the path -> CLI Error: + exit 1
reasoning: AC-6-016/BR-6-009 fail-loud, no stacktrace; one error class per infra module (ConfigError/StateError precedent); future 2nd adapter imports it without pulling file_delivery

decision: Removed Digest from ports.py import after verifying no other Protocol references it — DigestRenderer uses SummarizedItem only
reasoning: ruff F401 unused import; ADR-001 said verify before removing — confirmed safe
rejected: keep unused import to match ADR-001 wording

decision: FileDelivery: tmp_name set to None after successful os.replace so finally block skips unlink
reasoning: Pattern from json_store.py:save — prevents unlinking the renamed file

decision: StdoutDelivery accepts TextIO|None with default sys.stdout — injectable stream for testability
reasoning: Testable seam; design.md §API Design specifies this exact signature

decision: Added output_destination and output_path as frozen dataclass fields with defaults after state_path
reasoning: Design specifies exact field placement and defaults

decision: CLI wiring: BrokenPipeError handler uses os.dup2(devnull, sys.stdout.fileno()) then Exit(0); DeliveryError handler uses Exit(1)
reasoning: ADR-003 Flow 3 exact pattern; exit code 0 on broken pipe per spec Error States row

decision: Updated test_run_valid_config_exits_zero to remove 'pipeline not yet implemented' stdout assertion
reasoning: CLI now delivers via FileDelivery to ./digest.md; message no longer on stdout. AC-1-030 only requires exit 0 — assertion was testing implementation detail of the stub, not the AC
rejected: add separate stdout delivery test in test_cli.py
